title: Eurybia viscosa
intro: Eurybia viscosa, also known as sticky aster or sticky Eurybia, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to relieve congestion and soothe the mucous membranes. The bioactive constituents of Eurybia viscosa include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Eurybia viscosa is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Eurybia viscosa is classified as a species within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and reproductive characteristics. Eurybia viscosa is further classified as belonging to the genus Eurybia, a group of plants that are native to North America. The species is also known to be part of the tribe Astereae, which is a sub-group within the family Asteraceae. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Eurybia viscosa.
